The '''Hapan Battle Dragons''' were the premier [[Warship/Legends|warships]] used by the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]].

==Design and function==
Designed by [[Olanji/Charubah]], Battle Dragons became the primary capital ships of the [[Hapan Royal Navy]], supported by [[Nova-class battle cruiser|''Nova''-class battle cruisers]]. The Hapan fleet, led by [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]]'s crimson Battle Dragon ''[[Song of War]]'', contained at least 63 Battle Dragons, each representing one of the Consortium's worlds.

[[File:Battledragon-bp.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Battle Dragon [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]]

Battle Dragons featured a unique dual saucer-shaped hull 500 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in diameter with a central core connected by sixteen rotation support struts. The ships were armed with forty [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] and forty [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries affixed to rotating circular sections on the top and bottom hull plates. To compensate for slowly recharging weaponry, the disc would rotate to bring fresh guns to an enemy while the previously used guns recharged their capacitors, which the Hapans viewed as a more efficient use of firepower.<ref name="EGtVaV">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

In addition to the rotating weapon emplacements, Battle Dragons were armed with ten [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] tubes, [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors, dorsal- and ventral-mounted heavy triple ion cannon batteries and sixteen [[pulse mass mine]]s launched from four [[pulse mass generator tube]]s. Each Battle Dragon carried three squadrons of [[Miy'til starfighter]]s or [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] in onboard [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]] and a battalion of 500 troops. The ships required a crew of 1,200 and 190 gunners.

===Flaws===
Battle Dragons were discovered in battle to have had two serious flaws: firstly, the drive system could be damaged, crippling the vessel. Secondly, the designers intended for the Battle Dragon to be used ''en masse'', against very few targets. With that end in mind, they skimped on [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computers]], putting in only four of them.

[[File:CrackensThreatDossier_art.jpg|thumb|left|180px|General Cracken discusses the potential threat of the [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] cluster]]

The effect was to offer outstanding performance against a single target but was useless against a group of targets. Tactics for a lone Battle Dragon against a group were little more than the Dragon choosing a vector and throwing a wall of energy in that direction, hoping that random hits would allow the Dragon to escape, so poor were the few targeting computers' performance against a mass of targets.

===Tactics===
The Hapans used ion cannons to disable the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] of another [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]], such as an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], and then fired salvos of proton torpedoes. This was found to be extremely effective, especially when the Hapans attacked with multiple Battle Dragons, as witnessed during the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Dathomir]].

The pulse-mass mines simulated a mass shadow in space to prevented jumps into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] by enemy ships. It was impossible to shut down the mines until their power was depleted, so launching them could be as great a risk to a Battle Dragon as to the ships it was attempting to trap. The three [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of Miy'tils were deployed as a fighter screen to prevent heavy bombers penetrating the ion cannon defense. The ion cannon-based design is unique in capital ship design; but the design was a realistic response to the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster's]] level of advancement - their isolationist stance had resulted in their ion cannon technology being on par with, and perhaps even exceeding, the standard galactic level of achievement, but their turbolaser technology had lagged considerably. After joining the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|battle]] against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], the New Republic updated the Hapan fleet with modern turbolaser technology. [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] also helped upgrade several Battle Dragons with new [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] technology in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:BattleDragonsAtCoruscant-TEC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Hapan Battle Dragons surrounding [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].]]

When the Hapan Battle Dragons were introduced in ''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'', their weapons rotation system was described as simply a means of increasing their rate of fire. In the novel, it was indicated that this allowed Battle Dragons to fire three times more quickly than could Imperial starships. No mention was made of Hapan turbolasers recharging more slowly than their Imperial and New Republic counterparts until [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|RPG]] material introduced the concept.
